titleStmt.title: SII 9.354: original edition by R. Shama Sastry and N. Lakshminarayan Rao
No. 354. (A.R. No. 772 of 1917.) ON TWO PIECES OF A BROKEN HERO-STONE LYING NEAR THE ENTRANCE INTO THE VILLAGE SIRIVARA, HINDUPUR TALUK, SAME DISTRICT.

summary.p: This is dated Śaka 1243, Raudri, Bhādrapada, ba. 13, Sōmavāra corresponding to A.D. 1320 September 1, Monday in the reign of the Hoysaḷa king Vīra-Ballāḷadēva (III). It records the death of the Mahāsāmantādhipati Hiriya Bommeya-Nāyaka, son of Gaṅgeya-Nāyaka of Hoḷakallu in a fight against the cavalry of the chief (Manneya) of Mēlumāvu, when Aḷiya Mācheya-Daṇṇāyaka, the prime minister of the king was ruling at Penugoṇḍe. It records the gift of wet and garden lands beneath the Sirivara tank to those who were appointed to worship the hero-stone.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti . śrīmatu sakavarsa 1243 raudrisaṁvatsarada bhādrapada ba 13 sō . vīraballā
ḷadēvarsara pradāni Āḷiyamāceyadaṁṇnāykaru penugoṁḍeyalu rājyaṁ
māḍutiddu mēlumāvina maṁneyara mēle Etinaḍadalli śrīmanu
mahāsāvaṁtādhipati hoḷakalla gaṁgeyanāykara maga hiriyaboṁmeya
nāykaru kudureya daḷadamēle Ēṟi hoyikuṭṭadi raṇadalu biddu svargastarā
daru maṁgaḷa mahāśrī
śrīmatu I bīrakallapratiṣṭheya mūruvali pūjepunaskāra māḍuva sthanikarige sirivarada keṟeya hiṁ
de gadde ko 5 keyi ko 5 sarbbabhādhe parihāravāgi Ā māceyada(ṁ)ṇnāykaru dhārāpūrbba
kavāgi koṭṭa datti maṁgaḷa mahāśrī śrī śrī .
Digital edition of SII 9.354 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
